title: Implement Hardened Memory via Total Recall Orchestrator
description: Transition the memory management from best-effort asynchronous sync to a synchronous, guaranteed 'Memory Integrity Audit' using a local Total Recall orchestrator. This involves adding SessionEnd and Stop hooks and refactoring the SessionStart logic.

title: llama-monitor: TUI + Web dashboard
description: Implement both TUI (terminal) and Web (Flask) dashboards sharing the same data layer. TUI uses ASCII charts (60-second history on single line). Web uses Chart.js for smooth graphs. Both show server metrics, system resources, power draw, cost, and temperatures.

title: Add breadcrumb validation to memory audits
description: Add validation to check that memory breadcrumbs are properly linked across layers. Why: Orphaned memories and broken links reduce recall effectiveness. What to do: 1) Check every JRN_ entry has at least one breadcrumb link, 2) Check every REM_ entry links to relevant JRN_ entries, 3) Check every Beads issue linked still exists, 4) Report broken links in audit. Acceptance Criteria: Audit validates breadcrumbs and reports broken links. Type: task, Priority: 4

title: Add conditional save debounce for PostToolUse hook
description: Implement smart debouncing to prevent excessive saves during active editing. Why: Without debouncing, every Edit/Write triggers a save, causing noise and latency. What to do: 1) Track last tool use timestamp, 2) Wait 30-60s after last tool use before saving, 3) Track per-file or global changes, 4) Only save if significant changes detected. Design Notes: Debounce window 30-60s, track by file or globally. Acceptance Criteria: PostToolUse debounces saves during active editing. Type: task, Priority: 4

title: Review and document Remember plugin upgrade strategy
description: Document the strategy for handling Remember plugin updates to avoid repeated manual changes. Why: The Remember plugin is not ours and updates require manual tracking of custom changes. What to do: 1) Identify all custom modifications to Remember plugin scripts, 2) Document upgrade procedure, 3) Create automated diff checker for new versions, 4) Consider fork vs patch strategy. Acceptance Criteria: Upgrade procedure documented, diff checker created, strategy approved. Type: task, Priority: 4

title: Improve emergency capture with actual session extraction
description: Improve emergency capture to extract actual session state rather than just a static template. Why: Current emergency capture creates a static template with no actual session data. What to do: 1) Extract recent exchanges from session transcript, 2) Capture current file states, 3) Link to existing memories, 4) Include open Beads and pending tasks. Design Notes: Use session transcript API, include file diff snapshots. Acceptance Criteria: Emergency capture includes recent session activity, file states, and links to existing memories. Type: task, Priority: 3

title: Add memory health metrics to audit reports
description: Add metrics to track memory system health and identify decay over time. Why: The current audit reports pass/fail but doesn't track measurable metrics to identify slow decay or measure improvement. What to do: 1) Track days since last memory capture, 2) Track open Beads without corresponding REM_ entries, 3) Track JRN_ entries lacking breadcrumbs, 4) Add percentage metrics, 5) Report metrics in audit output as Health Score. Design Notes: Metrics should be lightweight, use simple thresholds, health score 100=ideal. Acceptance Criteria: Audit shows days since capture, beads without links, orphaned JRN entries, health score. Type: task, Priority: 3

title: Add Injection Phase verification after SessionStart recovery
description:
Add verification that the Remember plugin's recovery phase completed successfully before the session proceeds.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md specifies an 'Injection Phase' where updated memory state should be injected into the current session context. Currently, Total Recall runs recovery/consolidation but doesn't verify they completed or inject the memory into Claude's context.

## What to do
1. Add verification check for recovery completion (check for last-save.json marker)
2. Add verification check for consolidation completion (check for last-ndc.ts marker)
3. Report verification status in audit output
4. If verification fails, log warning but don't block session (fail-soft for robustness)

## Design Notes
- The Remember plugin runs recovery/consolidation in background
- We need to verify these completed before proceeding
- Should be fail-soft (warn but don't block) to avoid blocking sessions

## Acceptance Criteria
- SessionStart audit verifies recovery completed
- SessionStart audit verifies consolidation completed
- Verification status is reported in audit output

## Dependencies
- This blocks: nothing (standalone improvement)

title: Add PostToolUse hook with smart debouncing for significant tool use
description:
Add the PostToolUse hook to Total Recall, which was specified in MEMORY_RULES.md but never implemented.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md (line 71) specifies a PostToolUse hook: '(Optional) Provides a subtle nudge to verify/record recent decisions.' This hook was never added to hooks.json or memory_sync.py.

## What to do
1. Add PostToolUse hook entry to .claude/skills/total-recall/hooks.json
2. Add --post-tool command-line flag to memory_sync.py
3. Implement post_tool_use_mode() function to handle the event
4. The debounce logic is handled in imperium-astrale-eml

## Design Notes
- This is marked as optional in the spec
- Smart debouncing is critical to avoid noise during active editing
- Should only trigger on significant tool use, not every minor edit

## Acceptance Criteria
- PostToolUse hook is registered in hooks.json
- memory_sync.py handles --post-tool flag
- Handoff digest or journal entry is created after tool use (if significant)

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: nothing
- Blocks: imperium-astrale-eml (debounce logic)
notes: Note: This is the primary issue for PostToolUse implementation. The original issue imperium-astrale-u62 should be marked as duplicate/closed.

title: Add PostToolUse hook with smart debouncing for significant tool use
description:
Add the PostToolUse hook to Total Recall, which was specified in MEMORY_RULES.md but never implemented.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md (line 71) specifies a PostToolUse hook: '(Optional) Provides a subtle nudge to verify/record recent decisions.' This hook was never added to hooks.json or memory_sync.py.

## What to do
1. Add PostToolUse hook entry to .claude/skills/total-recall/hooks.json
2. Add --post-tool command-line flag to memory_sync.py
3. Implement post_tool_use_mode() function to handle the event
4. Add smart debouncing to only trigger on significant changes (e.g., file size change > threshold, or wait 30s after last tool use)
5. Group related tool uses (e.g., multiple edits to same file within debounce window)

## Design Notes
- This is marked as optional in the spec
- Smart debouncing is critical to avoid noise during active editing
- Should only trigger on significant tool use, not every minor edit
- Consider tracking 'last tool use' timestamp per file or globally

## Acceptance Criteria
- PostToolUse hook is registered in hooks.json
- memory_sync.py handles --post-tool flag
- Debounce logic prevents excessive saves during active editing
- Handoff digest or journal entry is created after tool use (if significant)

## Dependencies
- This blocks: imperium-astrale-u62 (the original PostToolUse issue which should be marked as duplicate/blocked)
close_reason: Superseded by imperium-astrale-hdx which has more complete requirements

title: llama-monitor: TUI dashboard
description: Implement terminal dashboard using urwid or rich library. Display server metrics, system resources, power draw, and cost in a full-screen interface. Include basic ASCII history chart (30-sec window, 1-sec resolution).
notes: Depends on core metrics collection being functional

title: llama-monitor: Core metrics collection (server + system)
description: Implement server_metrics.py (fetch /metrics, /slots, /props), system_metrics.py (CPU/GPU/Memory via psutil + pynvml), and aggregator.py (SQLite storage with raw + compressed tables). Include data retention management (compress raw to 1-min after 1 day).
notes: Updated design with per-process GPU monitoring via NVML's nvmlDeviceGetGraphicsRunningProcesses_v2(). Process detection matches llama-server.exe by name. Power calculation: gpu_power_process = gpu_power_total * (gpu_util_process / gpu_util_total). Idle baseline tracked when CPU+GPU < 5%. Database schema includes process_gpu_metrics_* tables and idle_baseline table.

title: Update memory_sync.py to use auto-generated IDs
description:
Update memory_sync.py to use the new ID generation helper function instead of inline ID creation.

## Why
The current code creates IDs inline in multiple places:
- handoff_mode():  (line 402)
- emergency_capture_mode(): timestamp-based filename generation

This defeats the purpose of the helper function and keeps the LLM burden high.

## What to do
1. Import or define the ID generation helper function
2. Replace all inline ID generation with calls to the helper
3. The helper should ONLY require: layer prefix + slug
4. Timestamp and full ID construction happen server-side

## Acceptance Criteria
- No inline f-string ID generation remains in memory_sync.py
- Helper function is called for all ID creation
- Tests show IDs are correctly generated with valid timestamps

title: Create ID generation helper function for memory entries
description:
Create a centralized helper function to generate memory entry IDs with the format [Layer]_[YYYYMMDD]_[HHMMSS]_[slug].

## Why
The current memory_sync.py generates IDs inline (e.g., ). This approach has problems:
- LLMs cannot reliably generate timestamps across 128k+ context windows
- Timestamps like 20260525_999999 are invalid
- Slugs may be misspelled, breaking the breadcrumb chain

## What to do
1. Create a Python helper function in memory_sync.py (or a new utils.py) that:
   - Takes: layer (REM/JRN), slug (string), optional content
   - Auto-generates: timestamp (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS), full ID
   - Returns: full ID string
   - Validates: slug format, timestamp validity
2. Update all ID generation calls to use this helper
3. Document the function for future use

## Design Decision
Store this in memory_sync.py for now since it's tightly coupled to the sync logic. If it grows, we can split to utils.py later.

title: Update Chrome plugin and troubleshoot marketplace refresh
description: The superpowers-marketplace marketplace failed to refresh with error: 'Failed to clone marketplace repository: Command git not found or is in an unsafe location'. Need to troubleshoot why git is not found in the plugin update context despite being in PATH. Also update the superpowers-chrome plugin to latest version.

title: Troubleshoot Chrome plugin for browser automation
description: The Chrome plugin (superpowers-chrome) is not working correctly. When attempting to navigate to pages, the browser opens but content extraction fails. The session files are created but appear empty or incomplete. Need to diagnose and fix the Chrome DevTools Protocol integration.

title: llama-monitor: Data retention & compression (configurable)
description: Implement configurable data retention with two compression levels. Compression settings: interval (1,2,5,10,15,20,30) + unit (second,minute,hour,day,week,month) for each level. Raw data collected at configurable interval (default 1 second). Level 1 compresses to 1-minute buckets, Level 2 compresses to 1-hour buckets.
notes: SQLite schema has _raw, _1m, _1h tables for system and server metrics. Compression: AVG() for rates, SUM() for counters. JSON fields for arrays (cores_percent, temps) averaged or stored as single value.

title: llama-monitor: Electricity cost calculator
description: Implement cost calculation based on GPU/CPU power draw, duration, and electricity rate. Store cost data in SQLite and display in TUI/web dashboard.

title: llama-monitor: Web dashboard
description: Implement Flask-based web server for remote monitoring. Includes / endpoint for HTML dashboard and /api/metrics for live updates. Simple vanilla JS/CSS frontend.
notes: Can be developed in parallel with TUI
close_reason: Superseded by imperium-astrale-4pp: TUI + Web dashboard combined task

title: llama-monitor: CLI stats command
description: Implement on-demand CLI command (python -m llama_monitor.cli_stats) that outputs current metrics in a readable format, similar to kubectl top.

title: Add PostToolUse hook for capturing decisions after tool use
description:
Add the PostToolUse hook to Total Recall, which was specified in MEMORY_RULES.md but never implemented.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md (line 71) specifies a PostToolUse hook: "(Optional) Provides a subtle nudge to verify/record recent decisions." This hook was never added to hooks.json or memory_sync.py.

## What to do
1. Add PostToolUse hook entry to .claude/skills/total-recall/hooks.json
2. Add --post-tool command-line flag to memory_sync.py
3. Implement post_tool_use_mode() function to handle the event
4. The hook should trigger after significant tool use (Edit/Write) to capture decisions while fresh

## Design Notes
- This is marked as optional in the spec
- Consider whether this adds value vs. potential noise/latency
- Should only trigger on significant tool use, not every minor edit

## Acceptance Criteria
- PostToolUse hook is registered in hooks.json
- memory_sync.py handles --post-tool flag
- Handoff digest or journal entry is created after tool use
